audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra appointed-times text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Sabbath, Passover/Unleavened Bread, firstfruits, Weeks, trumpet memorial, Day of Atonement, Booths, harvest justice, and sacred assembly rhythms.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: Appointed times of YHWH, which you shall proclaim as holy convocations—these are my appointed times.
Six days work shall be done, but on seventh day is Sabbath of Sabbath-rest, a holy convocation; no work you shall do. It is Sabbath to YHWH in all your dwellings.
These are appointed times of YHWH, holy convocations, which you shall proclaim in their appointed time.
In first month, on fourteenth of month between evenings, is Passover to YHWH.
On fifteenth day of this month is Festival of Unleavened Bread to YHWH; seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
On first day you shall have holy convocation; no laborious work you shall do.
You shall bring near fire-offering to YHWH seven days; on seventh day is holy convocation; no laborious work you shall do.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: When you come into land that I give you and reap its harvest, you shall bring first sheaf of your harvest to priest.
He shall wave sheaf before YHWH for your acceptance; on day after Sabbath priest shall wave it.
On day you wave sheaf, you shall make a complete male lamb, son of a year, as burnt offering to YHWH.
Its grain offering shall be two-tenths fine flour mixed with oil, fire-offering to YHWH, soothing scent; its drink offering shall be wine, fourth of a hin.
Bread, roasted grain, and fresh grain you shall not eat until this very day, until you bring offering of your Divine; age-long statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
You shall count for yourselves from day after Sabbath, from day you brought sheaf of wave offering, seven complete Sabbaths.
Until day after seventh Sabbath you shall count fifty days; then bring near new grain offering to YHWH.
From your dwellings bring two loaves as wave offering, two-tenths fine flour; leavened they shall be baked, firstfruits to YHWH.

With bread bring seven complete male lambs, sons of a year, one bull from herd, and two rams; they shall be burnt offering to YHWH, with grain offering and drink offerings, fire-offering, soothing scent to YHWH.
You shall make one male goat as sin/purification offering and two male lambs, sons of a year, as sacrifice of peace/well-being.
Priest shall wave them with bread of firstfruits as wave offering before YHWH, with two lambs; holy they shall be to YHWH for priest.
You shall proclaim on this very day a holy convocation for yourselves; no laborious work you shall do; age-long statute in all your dwellings throughout generations.

When you reap harvest of your land, you shall not finish edge of your field in reaping, and gleaning of your harvest you shall not gather; for poor and for sojourner you shall leave them. I am YHWH your Divine.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel, saying: In seventh month, on first of month, there shall be Sabbath-rest for you, memorial by trumpet blast, holy convocation.
No laborious work you shall do, and you shall bring near fire-offering to YHWH.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
But on tenth of this seventh month is Day of Atonement/Coverings; holy convocation it shall be for you, and you shall afflict/humble your souls/lives and bring near fire-offering to YHWH.
No work you shall do on this very day, for it is Day of Atonement/Coverings, to make atonement/covering for you before YHWH your Divine.
For every soul/life who is not humbled on this very day shall be cut off from its people.
Every soul/life who does any work on this very day, that soul/life I will destroy from midst of its people.
No work you shall do; age-long statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Sabbath of Sabbath-rest it is to you, and you shall humble your souls/lives; on ninth of month at evening, from evening to evening, you shall keep your Sabbath.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel, saying: On fifteenth day of this seventh month is Festival of Booths seven days to YHWH.
On first day is holy convocation; no laborious work you shall do.
Seven days you shall bring near fire-offering to YHWH; on eighth day you shall have holy convocation and bring near fire-offering to YHWH; it is solemn assembly; no laborious work you shall do.

These are appointed times of YHWH, which you shall proclaim as holy convocations, to bring near fire-offering to YHWH, burnt offering and grain offering, sacrifice and drink offerings, each matter on its day,
apart from Sabbaths of YHWH, apart from your gifts, apart from all your vows, and apart from all your freewill offerings that you give to YHWH.
Surely on fifteenth day of seventh month, when you gather produce of land, you shall celebrate festival of YHWH seven days; on first day Sabbath-rest and on eighth day Sabbath-rest.
On first day you shall take for yourselves fruit of splendid trees, branches of palms, boughs of leafy trees, and willows of stream, and rejoice before YHWH your Divine seven days.
You shall celebrate it as festival to YHWH seven days in year; age-long statute throughout your generations; in seventh month you shall celebrate it.
In booths you shall dwell seven days; every native in Israel shall dwell in booths,
so your generations may know that in booths I made children of Israel dwell when I brought them out from land of Egypt. I am YHWH your Divine.
Moses spoke appointed times of YHWH to children of Israel.

translation_notes: Moedim/appoined times are sacred rhythms proclaimed as holy convocations, not merely private observances.
Weekly Sabbath precedes annual festivals and frames time itself around cessation/rest.
Passover and Unleavened Bread preserve Exodus liberation memory.
Firstfruits and Weeks sanctify harvest before consumption and include leavened firstfruit bread.
Verse 22 interrupts festival ritual with field-edge justice: poor and sojourner must share harvest abundance.
Day of Atonement repeats soul-humbling and total rest; guard against self-hatred interpretations.
Booths culminates in rejoicing and embodied memory of wilderness dwelling after Egypt.
safety_note: Do not turn appointed times into legalistic status markers or shame those unable to observe them. The chapter joins sacred time to liberation memory, rest, harvest gratitude, communal joy, and material provision for poor and sojourner.
